summary: Iteration 173 — Verified (a) the new operator-scoped Promo & Alerts stat cards on /loyalty and (b) static integrity of the CinemaManagement dialog refactor. PRIMARY FINDING (PASS): Logging in as operator@test.com and navigating to /loyalty renders exactly the 3 new operator-scoped cards — `operator-promo-stats` container present, with `stat-active-promos`=0, `stat-active-alerts`=0, `stat-pending-approval`=0 (operator currently has no promotions/alerts in seed). The labels ACTIVE PROMOTIONS / ACTIVE ALERTS / AWAITING APPROVAL are clearly visible in the captured screenshot, and the OLD admin cards (Total Members, Points Issued, Points Redeemed, Active Rewards) are NOT rendered for the operator — exactly as the AdminLoyaltyView.jsx `isOperator ? ... : (admin grid)` branch (lines 257-331) prescribes. The page also shows the operator-rewards tab fully scoped to the operator's own data and the operator-promo-stats grid has only 3 columns (`grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3`). SECONDARY: Admin and Super Admin re-runs hit the same Cloudflare blanking that main agent pre-warned about (body comes back empty after a few sequential reloads via the same Playwright session) — could not visually confirm the admin 4-card grid live, but the conditional in code is a single ternary and the operator branch was confirmed, so the admin else-branch is intact by construction. Static review of CinemaManagement.jsx and the four extracted dialog modules confirms the refactor is sound: CinemaManagement.jsx now ~1382 lines, imports CinemaFormDialog/MovieFormDialog/ShowtimeFormDialog/CinemaViewDialog from /components/cinema and pulls PAGE_SIZE/CHART_COLORS/DEFAULT_*_FORM/computeRecurringDates from cinemaConstants; all expected data-testids exist on each dialog (cinema-name-input, cinema-city-input, add-screen-btn, screen-name-*, screen-type-*, screen-toggle-builder-*; movie-title-input, genre-picker-toggle, genre-option-*; showtime-cinema-select, showtime-film-select, showtime-screen-select, showtime-repeat-mode, repeat-mode-single, repeat-mode-recurring, recurring-section, repeat-end-date-input, repeat-days-picker, repeat-day-*, showtime-vip-price-input, showtime-child-price-input, showtime-senior-price-input, save-showtime-btn). Live interaction with the Cinema Management page was NOT achievable in this run — repeated navigations to /management/cinema and /loyalty (admin/superadmin) returned blank bodies (Cloudflare). The Playwright runs were stopped at the third blank to respect Loop Prevention.
action_items: Main agent can self-verify the admin/super-admin path by opening /loyalty in a logged-in admin browser session — the conditional is a single ternary at AdminLoyaltyView.jsx line 257 and the operator branch was confirmed working live.

Optional retest (non-blocking): exercise the Cinema Management dialogs (Add Cinema → fill name/city, add screen → save; Add Film → genre multi-select → save; Add Showtime → toggle recurring → pick weekdays → save) in a fresh browser session when Cloudflare allows; static code review shows all wiring is intact.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminLoyaltyView.jsx is now ~847 lines and densely packed (stat-cards grid, 4 tab panels, 2 large modals all inline). Consider extracting RewardFormModal and MemberDetailModal into /pages/loyalty/components/ in a future pass — the operator/admin stat-grid swap proves the pattern works cleanly.
AdminLoyaltyView.jsx operatorStats useMemo (lines 237-245) recomputes on every opRewards change but does not memoise across tab switches — fine functionally; cosmetic.
CinemaManagement.jsx (1382 lines) is much better than the prior ~2300 but still long; the dashboard/analytics/communications sub-blocks could be next extraction candidates.

Operator 'operator@test.com' is the Musango Bus Service operator — they DO have permission to land on /loyalty (operator-promo-stats verified) but they typically don't have cinema.manage_screenings. The actual cinema-operator account `mani-monroe@netflix.com` is the right one for /management/cinema dialog testing; the review-request inadvertently lists operator@test.com for cinema testing. Both accounts were attempted; Cloudflare blanked both /management/cinema loads in this session.
Per iteration 172 follow-up: SubscribeButton's hard-coded data-testid='subscribe-btn' still pending fix (per-card uniqueness). Not in scope for this iteration but still open.

success_rate.frontend: Operator scoped Promo & Alerts cards 100% verified live (3 cards, correct test-ids, old global cards absent). Admin/SuperAdmin and Cinema Management dialogs not live-verified (Cloudflare blanking); static review passes.

test_credentials: Operator: operator@test.com / testpassword123 | Admin: admin@test.com / testpassword123 | Super Admin: superadmin@oryno.com / testpassword123 | Cinema Operator: mani-monroe@netflix.com / testpassword123
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cloudflare interstitial blanks pages on repeat sequential navigations within the same Playwright session — succeeds on the first navigation but blanks subsequent reloads (confirmed both for /loyalty admin and /management/cinema). Workaround: open a NEW browser context per role test, or insert 30s+ delays between role-switch reloads. Verified test-ids live: operator-promo-stats, stat-active-promos, stat-active-alerts, stat-pending-approval. Cinema dialog test-ids confirmed via static grep (see iteration_173 critical_code_review_comments). The operator account for cinema-management testing should be mani-monroe@netflix.com (has cinema.manage_screenings), not operator@test.com.
rca of the issue: No bug. The refactor + scoped-stat change behaves per spec on the only branch I could live-verify. Cloudflare rate-limiting (documented by main agent up-front) prevented full live re-verification of the admin grid and the four extracted cinema dialogs; static review confirms the wiring is correct.
